question.text: Which of the following qualifications is accepted for the principal officer/proprietor of an individual investment adviser under SEBI IA Regulations?

answer.text: Any undergraduate degree from a recognized university
Only a Master's degree in Finance or Economics
Professional/graduate/PG degree in finance, commerce, economics, or related fields, OR CFA Charter, OR NISM PG Program (minimum 1 year)
Only CFA or CFP certification is accepted; academic degrees are not sufficient

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under SEBI IA Regulations 2013, the qualification requirement for investment advisers includes a professional, graduate, or post-graduate degree in finance, commerce, economics, banking, capital markets, insurance, actuarial science, business management, or related fields. Alternatively, a CFA Charter or completion of the NISM Post Graduate Program (minimum 1 year) is also accepted.
**The Trap:** A general undergraduate degree (e.g., BA in History) would not suffice — the degree must be in a finance-related field. Similarly, CFA/CFP is accepted but is not the only pathway — academic qualifications also work.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Qualification requirements are specific to NISM Series X-A. Note that persons "associated with advice" (not the principal) have a lower threshold — minimum graduate degree.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Ensure both you and your associated persons maintain valid NISM certifications at all times. The qualification is a one-time requirement; NISM certification is an ongoing obligation.
